rationale: Review 2’s main placement finding is broader and grounded in the diff: the public API is generic bialgebra/Hopf-algebra convolution machinery, not actual algebraic-group/functor-of-points infrastructure, so moving/splitting it would leave a better-organized PR. Review 1 catches a real but narrower namespace/privacy issue for `antipode_convMul_id`; Review 2’s PR-description finding is not confirmable from the shown diff and is not decisive.
